content: We can hypothesize that the dynamic prefrontal-hippocampal interaction during human memory retrieval acts as a implicit endogenous working memory retention gating mechanism: only retrieved episodic memory traces that meet current task-relevant processing thresholds are gated into sustained working memory retention, rather than all activated memories being retained. This framework can be translated into a new neural gating architecture for 2025 generation active learning episodic planning agents to mitigate their working memory capacity limits.
